description_long: This is a rustic wooden bucket with vertical wooden staves bound together by two dark metal or iron bands, one near the top and one near the bottom. The bucket features a thick, twisted rope handle attached to metal loops on either side, giving it a sturdy and functional appearance. The wood grain is visible, with some staves showing slight variations in color and texture, adding to its aged, handcrafted look. The interior is also wooden, matching the exterior, and the bucket is open at the top with no lid present. The rope handle arches above the bucket, suggesting it is designed for carrying or lowering into wells. This type of bucket would commonly be found in outdoor or garden settings, or in historical or rustic urban environments.
description: A rustic wooden bucket with a thick rope handle and metal reinforcement bands, featuring visible wood grain and an open top.
description_view_0: The bucket is seen from a slight angle, showing the rope handle arching over the top and the wood grain on the staves, with both metal bands visible.
description_view_1: This view is directly aligned with the handle, emphasizing the attachment points and the circular opening of the bucket.
description_view_2: The bucket is shown from the opposite side of view 0, again highlighting the rope handle and the continuous wood staves.
description_view_3: Similar to view 1, this view is aligned with the handle but from the opposite side, showing the symmetry of the handle and band placement.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: dark metal hoops at top and bottom
